title: Pan Seared Thai Serrano Ham Noodles
description: Rich and crispy Thai dish featuring serrano ham, this recipe is a show-off dish that combines the savory flavors of Thailand with the salty taste of serrano ham, all cooked to perfection in a copper pot.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a copper pot over medium-high heat.
Add the thinly sliced serrano ham to the pot and cook until crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the ham from the pot and set aside on a paper towel-lined plate.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the pot and stir in the Thai red curry paste.
Cook the curry paste for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, brown sugar, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the rice noodles to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, until they are well coated with the sauce.
Return the crispy serrano ham to the pot and stir to combine with the noodles and sauce.
Transfer the noodles to a serving platter and garnish with chopped fresh cilantro.
